subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 17
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, community, and health for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is known for having a trunk?
questions.explanation: Elephants are the only living animals that have a trunk, which they use for breathing, smelling, and grasping.
questions.hint: It is the largest land animal.
questions.objective: Identify common animals and their characteristics.

questions.q: What is the capital city of the United Kingdom?
questions.explanation: London is the capital and largest city of both England and the United Kingdom.
questions.hint: It is famous for Big Ben.

questions.q: Which part of a plant absorbs sunlight?
questions.explanation: Leaves contain chlorophyll, which helps plants absorb sunlight to make food.
questions.hint: They are usually green and flat.

questions.q: What do we call the imaginary line that divides Earth into two halves?
questions.explanation: The equator is an imaginary line around the middle of the Earth at 0 degrees latitude.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy snack choice?
questions.explanation: Apples are high in fiber and vitamins, making them a much healthier snack than sugary treats.
questions.hint: It is a fruit that can be red, green, or yellow.

questions.explanation: An architect is a person who designs buildings and advises on their construction.
Humans breathe in oxygen and exhale carbon dioxide.

questions.q: Which of these is a liquid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Water is a liquid at room temperature, whereas rock and metal are solids.
questions.hint: You need this to stay hydrated.

questions.q: What is the largest land animal on Earth?
questions.explanation: The African elephant is the largest living terrestrial animal.

questions.q: Which force pulls objects toward the ground?
questions.explanation: Gravity is the force that pulls all objects toward the center of the Earth.
questions.hint: It keeps your feet on the ground.
